title: Keep FFI buffers owned until cancellation is settled
prompt:
The C++ bridge in `src/buffer_transfer.cpp` consumes an owned buffer and lends its bytes to a C transport until submission is rejected or the transport finishes with them. Its ordinary rejection, completion, and fully suppressed cancellation paths work, but cancellation that leaves a completion callback pending releases the buffer while the C transport may still read it; the eventual callback then releases the same buffer again. Repair the ownership transition at its root.

Preserve the public API and the serialized callback contract in `include/buffer_transfer.h`. `buffer_bridge::submit` consumes every valid `ffi_owned_buffer`, including when submission is rejected or bridge-state allocation fails. Rejection guarantees no callback and must release before returning. An accepted submission must retain the exact original data pointer, size, owner, and release function until the transport relinquishes its loan. Every completion status relinquishes it and releases exactly once. `FFI_CANCEL_CALLBACK_SUPPRESSED` means `cancel` has synchronously relinquished the loan and guarantees no callback, so cancellation must release exactly once before returning. `FFI_CANCEL_CALLBACK_PENDING` means the transport may still read the bytes and guarantees one later callback, so the bytes must remain owned until that late callback releases them exactly once.

Do not copy or modify payload bytes, replace the caller's release function, add buffer allocation, retry submission or cancellation, introduce ownership registries, change callback timing, or change the serialized execution model. Make the focused correction in `src/buffer_transfer.cpp`. Do not modify the protected header, Makefile, or tests, and do not weaken the strict C++17 compiler flags. Run `make test`; it is offline and deterministic.

full_distill.primary_skill: repair exact-once ownership transfer across a serialized C callback boundary while preserving cancellation acknowledgement and late-callback lifetime semantics
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the strict C++17 build succeeds and rejection, normal completion, and callback-suppressed cancellation pass, but the deterministic pending-callback case observes release during cancel while the transport still holds the loan and a second release when the promised late callback runs
full_distill.success_contract: each consumed buffer is released exactly once with its original data/size/owner tuple: immediately on rejection, on ordinary completion, during callback-suppressed cancellation, or only at the promised late completion when cancellation reports callback pending; payload bytes and callback sequencing remain unchanged
adversarial_factors: the release audit deliberately keeps storage mapped and counts every invocation, exposing a double release deterministically without relying on allocator crashes or sanitizers
the pending-callback fixture reads the payload after cancel returns, pinning that the transport's loan remains valid until the late callback
separate rejected, successful, callback-suppressed, and callback-pending transports pin all ownership exits rather than accepting a blanket leak or blanket early release
release checks include the exact original owner, data pointer, and size so replacing the release callback or reconstructing the buffer cannot masquerade as correct transfer
protected API and build inputs retain the C-compatible callback table, serialized timing contract, no-payload-copy rule, and strict warning-as-error C++17 build
